item.title: Overview
item.description:
IDPicker helps students evaluate strengths, explore academic and career options, and get guided recommendations.

Primary User Roles

  • Student
  • Advisor
  • Parent

Panel Entry Points

  • Student Panel: main user learning and recommendation workspace
  • Advisor Panel: supervision, student support, and sub-advisor administration
  • Parent Panel: visibility for linked students

Student Account Types

  • Linked Student: account linked to parent visibility model
  • Individual Student: independent account with direct access only

Image placeholder: Add screenshot or diagram showing where each role signs in.

item.title: Quick Start
item.description:
Step 1: Open the Login Page

Use the correct login URL for your environment.

Image placeholder: Add screenshot of login page with visible URL in browser bar.

Step 2: Sign In

  1. Enter email/username.
  2. Enter password.
  3. Click Sign In.

For OAuth login, click the provider button (for example Google) if enabled.

Image placeholder: Add screenshot of filled sign-in form (mask sensitive data).

Step 3: Create First Item

After login, open Dashboard and start the first guided workflow:

item.title: Access URLs
item.description:
Use this page as the single source of truth for platform URLs.

Production URLs

Purpose URL
Main Platform Login https://app.idpicker.com/login
Student Panel https://app.idpicker.com/student/dashboard
Advisor Panel https://app.idpicker.com/advisor/dashboard
Parent Panel https://app.idpicker.com/parent/dashboard
Help Center https://app.idpicker.com/help-center

Staging/Test URLs

Purpose URL
API Base https://testapi.idpicker.com/api
Language Preference API https://testapi.idpicker.com/api/set-language-preference/

URL Validation Checklist

  1. Login URL is reachable.
  2. Role-based panel redirects are correct.
  3. Unauthorized role access is blocked.

Image placeholder: Add screenshot of browser tabs showing each panel URL after login.

item.title: System Requirements
item.description:
Minimum Requirements

List CPU, RAM, disk, and network requirements.

Supported Platforms

List supported operating systems and versions.

Dependencies

List required runtimes, tools, and services.

item.title: Setup
item.description:
Install Steps

Document installation commands or installer steps.

Configure Settings

Describe required configuration values.

Validate Installation

Provide checks to confirm successful setup.

item.title: Common Tasks
item.description:
Task 1

Describe purpose, steps, and expected result.

Task 2

Describe purpose, steps, and expected result.

Task 3

Describe purpose, steps, and expected result.

item.title: Best Practices
item.description:
Recommended Workflow

Explain the preferred sequence of actions.

Security Tips

List secure usage guidelines.

Performance Tips

Describe ways to keep usage efficient.

item.title: Configuration Reference
item.description:
This page documents high-impact configuration values used across IDPicker modules.

1. Core Environment Variables

Key Typical Value (Local) Used By Purpose
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL http://localhost:8000/api Student, Advisor, Parent, Admin dashboards Base API URL for frontend requests
NODE_ENV development / production All frontends Runtime mode and behavior switches

Notes:

  1. Frontends append role-specific paths in API clients where needed.
  2. Some shared endpoints remove role suffix at runtime in client helper logic.

2. Local Frontend Dev URLs

App Typical Local URL
Student module frontend http://localhost:3000
Admin dashboard http://localhost:3001
Advisor dashboard http://localhost:3002
Parent dashboard http://localhost:3003

3. Authentication Cookies (Frontend)

Role Cookie Name Purpose
Student authToken Student API authorization token
Advisor authTokenAdvisor Advisor API authorization token
Parent authTokenParent Parent API authorization token
Admin authTokenAdmin Admin API authorization token

Operational notes:

item.title: Troubleshooting
item.description:
Use these playbooks when users report blocked workflows.

Playbook 1: Cannot Sign In

Symptoms:

  • Invalid credentials error
  • Redirect loop to login
  • Role mismatch message

Checks:

  1. Confirm correct role URL.
  2. Confirm account exists and is active.
  3. Confirm email verification status.
  4. Clear cookies and retry login.

Escalate when:

  • Multiple users fail with same error pattern.
  • Login endpoint responds with server errors.

Playbook 2: Form Cannot Submit

Symptoms:

  • Submit button fails silently
  • Validation error persists
  • Response not saved

Checks:

item.title: FAQ
item.description:
Access and Accounts

1. I cannot log in. What should I check first?

  1. Confirm you are using the correct panel URL for your role.
  2. Re-enter username/email and password carefully.
  3. Check whether your email verification is pending.
  4. Use password reset if you suspect credential mismatch.

2. Why do I get redirected back to login?

Most common causes:

  • Expired or invalid auth cookie
  • Wrong role token used on another panel
  • Backend session/token invalidation

Fix:

item.title: 01. Authentication Guide
item.description:
This guide explains exactly how a student signs in, registers, verifies email, and recovers account access.

What You Need Before Starting

  • Student login URL from Access URLs
  • Valid email address
  • Stable internet connection
  • Access to your email inbox (and spam folder)

A) Sign In with Email and Password (Click-by-Click)

  1. Open the student login page.
  2. Click the Email field.
  3. Type your registered email.
  4. Click the Password field.
  5. Type your password.
  6. Optional: Click the eye icon to reveal password and verify typing.
  7. Click Sign In.
  8. Wait for redirect.

Expected result:

item.title: 02. Dashboard Guide
item.description:
The dashboard is the student home screen. This guide explains where to click first and how to use each block.

Open Dashboard

  1. Sign in successfully.
  2. Wait for automatic redirect to dashboard.
  3. Confirm left sidebar is visible.
  4. Confirm core widgets/cards are loaded.

Recommended First 5 Clicks After Login

  1. Click Notifications preview to check latest updates.
  2. Click Assigned Forms section and open pending form (if any).
  3. Click Resume/Continue assessment prompt (if shown).
  4. Click Recommendations from sidebar.
  5. Return to dashboard and check promoted/random programs.

Dashboard Blocks and How to Use Them

1) Assessment Reports Block

  1. Locate assessment report area.
  2. If report exists, click view/download action.
  3. If locked or unavailable, complete required assessments first.

2) Assigned Forms Block

  1. Locate Assigned Forms.
  2. Click open form on a pending entry.
  3. Complete in dedicated form page.
  4. Return to dashboard after submit.

3) Organization Invitations Block

  1. Open invitation card.
  2. Review organization name and context.
  3. Click Accept or Decline.
  4. Wait for confirmation.

4) Notifications Block

  1. Review unread indicator.
  2. Click relevant notification-driven module.
  3. Return and continue through remaining items.

5) Program Discovery Blocks

  • Promoted programs: prioritized highlights.
  • Random programs: exploratory cards.
  1. Click a program card.
  2. Review core fields.
  3. Continue exploration in University Search for filtering.

Incomplete Assessment Resume Flow

When shown:

item.title: 03. Assessment Guide
item.description:
This guide explains how students start, continue, submit, and review assessments.

Open Self Assessment

  1. From sidebar, click Self Assessment.
  2. Wait for cards to load.
  3. Confirm default cards (Personality and Career) are visible.
  4. Check for organization/platform custom cards if assigned.

Start a New Assessment

  1. Click the card you want (for example Personality).
  2. Click Start.
  3. Read question instructions.
  4. Select or type answer.
  5. Click Next to continue.
  6. Repeat until all required questions are completed.
  7. Click Submit.

Continue an Incomplete Assessment

  1. Return to Self Assessment list.
  2. Find card showing Continue state.
  3. Click Continue.
  4. Resume from saved position.
  5. Complete and submit.

Retake an Assessment

If retake is allowed:

item.title: 04. Recommendation Guide
item.description:
This guide explains how students use recommendations, switch guidance track, and download reports.

Open Recommendations

  1. In sidebar, click Recommendations.
  2. Wait for profile-based track to load.
  3. Read page header to confirm active mode:
    • Career Recommendations
    • University Recommendations

Understand Active Track

Track is usually determined by profile data:

  • Grade 9-12 tends to map to Career track.
  • University-level or unknown fallback tends to map to University track.

Use Recommendation List (Click-by-Click)

  1. Scroll through recommendation cards/list.
  2. Open each recommendation detail.
  3. Compare options using your assessment outcomes.
  4. Save or note top choices externally if needed.

Switch Guidance Track

  1. Scroll to Guidance Track section.
  2. Click switch action:
    • Switch to Career
    • Switch to University
  3. Wait for save confirmation.
  4. Re-check header and recommendation content.

Download Full Report (When Available)

  1. Confirm Download Report button is visible.
  2. Click Download My Report.
  3. Wait for preparation state to finish.
  4. New tab opens with generated report link.

If button is not visible:

item.title: 05. Personality Guide
item.description:
This guide explains exactly how students read personality results and export reports.

Prerequisite Check

  1. In sidebar, click Personality.
  2. If assessment is incomplete, you will see a completion prompt.
  3. Click Complete Assessment and finish personality self-assessment first.

Open Personality Analysis

  1. After completion, return to Personality.
  2. Wait for loading to finish.
  3. Confirm radar chart is visible.
  4. Confirm trait cards are visible below chart.

Read Radar Chart (Click-by-Click)

  1. Hover each radar axis point.
  2. Identify stronger and weaker dimensions.
  3. Cross-check chart with trait card scores.

Read Trait Cards

For each card:

item.title: 06. AI Mentorship Guide
item.description:
Mentor area includes AI mentor personalities and advisor chat (if advisor is linked).

Open Mentor Hub

  1. In sidebar, click Mentor.
  2. Wait for mentor cards to load.
  3. Check quota indicator (if visible).

Start Chat with AI Mentor (Click-by-Click)

  1. Browse mentor cards.
  2. Click Select/Open on preferred mentor.
  3. Chat screen opens.
  4. Click message input.
  5. Type your question.
  6. Click send action.
  7. Continue follow-up prompts.

Return to Mentor List

  1. In chat view, click Back to Mentors.
  2. Select another mentor if needed.

Open Advisor Chat (If Linked)

  1. In mentor main page, locate advisor section.
  2. Check advisor summary (name, unread count, last message info).
  3. Click advisor chat action.
  4. Continue conversation.
  5. Use back action to return.

If advisor section says no advisor:

item.title: 07. University Search Guide
item.description:
This guide explains exactly how students search, filter, and compare universities/programs.

Open University Search

  1. In sidebar, click University Search.
  2. Wait for filter panel and results area to load.
  3. Confirm default result set appears.

Basic Search (Keyword)

  1. Click search input.
  2. Type university or program keyword.
  3. Pause briefly (search is debounced).
  4. Review refreshed results.

Filter Search (Click-by-Click)

Category filter

  1. Click Category dropdown.
  2. Select one category.
  3. Wait for results refresh.

Degree filter

  1. Click Degree dropdown.
  2. Select degree type.
  3. Wait for results refresh.

Language filter

  1. Click Language dropdown.
  2. Select instruction language.
  3. Wait for results refresh.

Page size

  1. Click Page Size.
  2. Choose 6, 12, or 24.
  3. Results repaginate automatically.

Pagination and Load More Behavior

  1. Scroll through current page results.
  2. Use pagination/load action to fetch next set.
  3. Continue until target programs are found.

Important:

item.title: 08. Resources Guide
item.description:
This guide explains how students find and download advisor-shared learning resources.

Open Resources

  1. In sidebar, click Resources.
  2. Wait for resource list/grid to load.
  3. Confirm cards or rows are visible.

Search Resources by Keyword

  1. Click search input.
  2. Type keyword from title, description, or file name.
  3. Review filtered list.

Filter and Sort (Click-by-Click)

File type filter

  1. Click File Type filter.
  2. Select a type.
  3. Verify list narrows.

Size filter

  1. Click Size filter.
  2. Select Small/Medium/Large.
  3. Verify list updates.

Visibility filter

  1. Click Visibility filter.
  2. Choose All / Visible / Assigned.
  3. Verify list updates.

Sort

  1. Click Sort dropdown.
  2. Choose date/name/size order.
  3. Verify ordering refresh.

View mode

  1. Click Grid or List toggle.
  2. Confirm presentation changes.

Download a Resource

  1. Locate target resource card/row.
  2. Click Download.
  3. Wait for browser download start.
  4. Confirm file appears in downloads folder.

If Download Fails

  1. Retry download once.
  2. Refresh page and retry.
  3. Confirm session is still active.
  4. Open Help Center ticket if persistent.

Common Student Questions

Q: I cannot find file shared by advisor. A: Check visibility filter and search by exact file name.

item.title: 09. Help Center Guide
item.description:
Help Center is where students create support tickets and track responses.

Open Help Center

  1. In sidebar, click Help Center.
  2. Wait for page to load tabs.
  3. Confirm New Ticket and My Tickets tabs are visible.

Create Ticket (Click-by-Click)

  1. Ensure New Ticket tab is selected.
  2. Click Subject input and type issue title.
  3. Click Category dropdown and select category.
  4. Click Priority dropdown and select urgency.
  5. Click Description and write detailed issue context.
  6. Click Submit.
  7. Wait for success confirmation.

Category and Priority Guidance

Category:

item.title: 10. Subscription Guide
item.description:
This guide explains how students identify free-plan limits and use upgrade paths.

Understand Feature Limits

Free-plan limits can apply to:

  • Mentor chat usage
  • Assessment/recommendation usage counters
  • Full report download availability

Where Students See Limit Signals

  1. Open a quota-sensitive module (Mentor, Assessments, Recommendations).
  2. Locate quota indicator or blocked state.
  3. Read remaining usage and limit label.

Upgrade Flow from Feature Context

  1. Reach a gated/limited action.
  2. Click Upgrade or equivalent CTA.
  3. Follow the presented flow for plan upgrade.

Note:

item.title: 11. Account and Settings Guide
item.description:
This guide explains how students edit profile, manage parent links, switch guidance track, and delete account safely.

A) Edit Profile (Click-by-Click)

  1. In sidebar, click Profile.
  2. Click Edit Profile.
  3. Update fields as needed:
    • First name
    • Last name
    • Bio
    • Location
    • Job title
    • Gender
    • Instagram URL
    • X URL
  4. Click Save.
  5. Wait for success confirmation.

If save fails:

  • Recheck required fields and URL formats.
  • Retry once after page refresh.

B) Switch Recommendation Track from Settings

  1. In sidebar, click Settings.
  2. Locate Guidance Track section.
  3. Click switch action (Career/University).
  4. Wait for saved confirmation.
  5. Open Recommendations and confirm new mode.

C) Link Parent Account

  1. Stay on Settings page.
  2. Locate Parent Linking section.
  3. Click parent email input.
  4. Enter parent email.
  5. Click Send/Request Link.
  6. Wait for confirmation.

Possible outcomes:

item.title: Advisor Login and Dashboard
item.description:
This guide covers advisor access, role-based session behavior, and first dashboard checks.

A) Advisor Login (Click-by-Click)

  1. Open advisor login URL from Access URLs.
  2. Click Email field and enter advisor email.
  3. Click Password field and enter password.
  4. Click Sign In.
  5. Wait for session initialization.
  6. Confirm redirect to dashboard.

Expected result:

  • Advisor session is loaded with role and permission set.
  • User lands on advisor dashboard.

If login fails:

  • Re-check email/password.
  • Confirm account status is active.
  • Use request/reset password flow if needed.

B) First-Time Password Setup

For invited advisors/sub-advisors:

item.title: 12. Language Guide
item.description:
Student panel supports English and Turkish.

Switch Language (Normal Case)

  1. Open sidebar.
  2. Click language switcher.
  3. Select target language.
  4. Wait for UI text refresh.
  5. Navigate to another page to confirm persistence.

Switch Language During In-Progress Assessment

If an assessment is in progress, a confirmation dialog can appear.

  1. Select new language.
  2. Read warning: in-progress assessment may be reset.
  3. Click Cancel to keep current language and progress.
  4. Or click Confirm to switch language and reset in-progress state.
  5. Page reloads with selected language.

Validation Checklist

After language switch, verify:

item.title: Students and Bulk Import
item.description:
This guide covers student search/filter, profile access, group assignment, and bulk import.

A) Open Students List

  1. Sign in as advisor.
  2. In sidebar, click Students.
  3. Wait for student table/cards to load.

B) Search and Filter Students (Click-by-Click)

  1. Click search input.
  2. Type name or email keyword.
  3. Apply filters as needed (groups, chat status, assessment signals, date, location, etc.).
  4. Apply sorting (name, email, joined date, and other available fields).
  5. Review narrowed result list.

C) Open Student Detail

  1. Click a student row/card.
  2. Open student detail page.
  3. Review profile and activity context.

D) Update Student Group Membership

  1. From Students list, open group assignment action.
  2. Check/uncheck target groups.
  3. Save membership update.
  4. Confirm changes in student row/detail.

E) Bulk Import Students from Excel

  1. In Students page, click Bulk Students/Import.
  2. Upload .xlsx or .xls file.
  3. Wait for preview parsing.
  4. Validate required columns exist:
    • email
    • first_name
    • last_name
  5. Select rows to import.
  6. Click Bulk Create/Import.
  7. Wait for success summary.

Import Validation Notes

  • Max file size is limited.
  • Invalid file type is rejected.
  • Rows with missing required fields are skipped or flagged.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Import file uploaded but no preview appears. A: Confirm Excel format and header names match expected fields.

item.title: 13. Certificates and Application Documents
item.description:
This guide covers two high-value student workflows: earned certificates and application document submission.

A) Certificates (Click-by-Click)

  1. In sidebar, click Certificates.
  2. Wait for cards to load.
  3. Use search input to find by template/subject/issuer.
  4. Use filter tabs: All, Certificate, Badge.
  5. Click a certificate card to open detail dialog.
  6. Review:
    • Template name
    • Subject
    • Issuer
    • Issue date
    • Status
  7. Click Copy ID to copy verification identifier.
  8. If download link exists, click Download.

B) Application Documents Setup

  1. In sidebar, click Application Documents.
  2. First-time users: choose nationality (Turkish/International).
  3. Collection is created.
  4. Wait for step categories and requirements to load.

C) Upload Documents by Step (Click-by-Click)

  1. Select step/category tab.
  2. Select required document type.
  3. Choose file from device.
  4. Add optional notes.
  5. Click Upload.
  6. Repeat for all required items in current step.
  7. Mark step complete when requirements are satisfied.
  8. Continue until all steps are complete.

D) Review Status and Fix Invalid Files

Each document may show:

item.title: Groups Management
item.description:
Groups help advisors organize students into cohorts for assignment and communication.

A) Open Groups

  1. In sidebar, click Groups.
  2. Wait for groups list to load.

B) Create Group (Click-by-Click)

  1. Click Create Group.
  2. Enter group name.
  3. Enter optional description.
  4. Select student members.
  5. Click Save.
  6. Confirm new group appears in list.

C) Edit Group

  1. Open target group.
  2. Click Edit.
  3. Update name/description/members.
  4. Save changes.
  5. Confirm updated values in list/detail.

D) Delete Group

  1. Open group actions.
  2. Click Delete.
  3. Confirm deletion prompt.
  4. Verify group is removed.

E) Best Practice for Group Strategy

  • Create groups by grade, track, or campaign objective.
  • Keep names consistent and searchable.
  • Review membership regularly.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: I cannot add a student to group. A: Check permission scope or reload student source list.

item.title: 14. Assigned Forms Guide
item.description:
Assigned forms are organization/advisor forms that students complete from dashboard or direct link.

Open Assigned Form from Dashboard

  1. Open Dashboard.
  2. Locate Assigned Forms block.
  3. Click Open Form on pending item.
  4. Wait for form page to load.

Understand Form Header

At top, you may see:

  • Organization banner image
  • Form title
  • Form description
  • Editability state

Complete Form Fields (Click-by-Click)

For each field:

  1. Read label and required indicator.
  2. Click input control.
  3. Enter/select response.
  4. Continue to next field.

Supported field patterns include text, textarea, number, email, dropdown, radio, checkbox, date/time/datetime, and rating.

item.title: Forms and Responses
item.description:
This guide covers managing advisor forms, publication state, and responses.

A) Open Forms Module

  1. In sidebar, click Forms.
  2. Wait for forms list and summary counters.

B) Search and Filter Forms

  1. Use search input for form title/metadata.
  2. Filter by status (draft, published, archived, all).
  3. Review counts (total forms, published forms, total responses).

C) Create New Form

  1. Click Create New Form.
  2. Enter title and description.
  3. Add/edit form fields in builder.
  4. Save as draft.
  5. Publish when ready.

D) Manage Existing Forms

  1. Open form from list.
  2. Edit structure/content if still editable.
  3. Publish/unpublish per workflow.
  4. Delete form using action menu and confirmation.

E) Review Responses

  1. Open form.
  2. Click Responses.
  3. Review respondent list and submitted answers.
  4. Open individual response detail for deeper review.

F) Preview Form

  1. Open form actions.
  2. Click Preview.
  3. Validate visual and field behavior before distribution.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Response count does not update immediately. A: Refresh list and confirm responses endpoint load completed.

item.title: Resources Management
item.description:
Resources lets advisors upload, organize, and assign files to students.

A) Open Resources

  1. In sidebar, click Resources.
  2. Wait for resource list/grid.

B) Upload a Resource (Click-by-Click)

  1. Click Upload Resource.
  2. Choose file from local device.
  3. Enter metadata (name/description/category as available).
  4. Select visibility/assignment mode.
  5. Click Upload.
  6. Confirm item appears in list.

C) Filter and Sort Resources

  1. Use search input (filename/title).
  2. Apply file-type filter.
  3. Apply size and visibility filters.
  4. Choose sort mode (date/name/size).
  5. Toggle list/grid view if needed.

D) Assign Resource to Students

  1. Open resource actions.
  2. Click Assign to Students.
  3. Select one or more students.
  4. Save assignment.
  5. Confirm visibility reflects assignment mode.

E) Delete Resource

  1. Open resource actions.
  2. Click Delete.
  3. Confirm modal.
  4. Verify resource is removed.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Upload failed unexpectedly. A: Verify file size/type limits and retry.

item.title: Support Tickets
item.description:
Support Tickets page helps advisors track and resolve student-reported issues.

A) Open Support Tickets

  1. In sidebar, click Support Tickets.
  2. Wait for ticket list and summary cards.

B) Filter and Prioritize

  1. Filter by status (pending, in_progress, resolved, closed).
  2. Filter by priority (low, medium, high, urgent).
  3. Sort list for triage order.
  4. Open most urgent unresolved tickets first.

C) Review Ticket Details

  1. Click ticket row/card.
  2. Read subject and full description.
  3. Check user details and timestamps.
  4. Identify category and visibility context.

D) Update Ticket Status

  1. Open status control.
  2. Set new status (for example in_progress).
  3. Save update.
  4. Confirm status badge changed in list.

E) Add Resolution Message

  1. Open resolution input.
  2. Write clear resolution notes.
  3. Save and mark resolved when done.
  4. Close ticket when finalized.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Status changed but not persisted. A: Reopen ticket and verify update request succeeded.

item.title: Notifications
item.description:
Notifications allows advisors to send targeted announcements to students.

A) Open Notifications

  1. In sidebar, click Notifications.
  2. Wait for notification history list.

B) Create New Notification (Click-by-Click)

  1. Click Create Notification.
  2. Enter title.
  3. Enter message body.
  4. Choose target type:
    • All students
    • Selected users
    • Selected groups
  5. If users/groups selected, choose recipients.
  6. Click Send/Create.
  7. Confirm new notification appears in history.

C) Review Delivery Summary

In notification list, verify:

  • Target type
  • Created date/time
  • Total recipients
  • Read count
  • Unread count

D) Filter History

  1. Apply target-type filters.
  2. Search for a title or campaign keyword.
  3. Review historical effectiveness.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Notification sent to wrong audience. A: Recheck target type and recipient selection before sending.

item.title: Certificates and Application Documents
item.description:
This guide covers advisor certificate operations and application document review.

A) Certificates Workflow

  1. In sidebar, open Certificates.
  2. Review available certificate templates/items.
  3. Open template/detail.
  4. Configure issuance data as needed.
  5. Send/issue certificate to selected student(s).
  6. Confirm status (sent, pending, failed or equivalent).

B) Application Documents Overview

  1. In sidebar, open Application Documents.
  2. Review student collections list.
  3. Filter by status and review state.
  4. Open a student collection detail.

C) Review Student Documents (Click-by-Click)

  1. Open specific step/category in student collection.
  2. Open uploaded file metadata and preview/download.
  3. Set review status:
    • Verified
    • Invalid
    • Pending (if re-evaluation needed)
  4. If invalid, provide reason/comment.
  5. Save review outcome.

D) Collection-Level Status Updates

  1. Review completion progress.
  2. If complete and valid, move toward approval state.
  3. If missing/invalid files remain, return with revision request.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Student uploaded file but not visible. A: Refresh collection detail and verify step filter.

item.title: Sub-Advisor Management
item.description:
This guide explains how advisors create sub-advisors, assign permissions, and validate delegated access.

A) Open Sub-Advisor Area

  1. Sign in as advisor.
  2. Open sidebar.
  3. Click Sub-Advisor or Sub-Advisors module.
  4. Wait for list and permission context to load.

B) Create Sub-Advisor (Click-by-Click)

  1. Click Create Sub-Advisor.
  2. Fill required fields:
    • Full name
    • Email
    • Assigned scope/group access
    • Permission set
  3. Review selected permissions carefully.
  4. Click Save/Create.
  5. Confirm invitation/account provisioning success.

C) Configure Permissions

  1. Open sub-advisor record from list.
  2. Edit permission matrix (module/action level, if available).
  3. Adjust student/group visibility scope.
  4. Save changes.
  5. Verify effective access with test sign-in.

D) Validate Restricted Visibility

  1. Sign in as sub-advisor account.
  2. Open Students and Groups modules.
  3. Confirm only authorized cohorts are visible.
  4. Confirm restricted actions are hidden/disabled where expected.

E) Deactivate or Revoke Access

  1. Open sub-advisor account.
  2. Change status to inactive (or revoke access action).
  3. Save change.
  4. Verify sub-advisor can no longer access protected modules.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Sub-advisor cannot see any students. A: Check group/visibility assignment in permission scope.

item.title: Enrollment, Family Chat, and Assessments
item.description:
This guide combines operational modules often handled together by advisors.

A) Enrollment Requests

  1. In sidebar, click Enrollment Requests.
  2. Wait for request list to load.
  3. Open a request detail.
  4. Review requester and related context.
  5. Click Approve or Deny.
  6. Confirm status update in list.

B) Family Chat

  1. In sidebar, click Family Chat.
  2. Choose chat scope/tab (student or parent context).
  3. Open conversation.
  4. Type response in message input.
  5. Click Send.
  6. Confirm message appears in thread.

C) Student Chat Module

  1. Open Students Chat page if separate route is available.
  2. Select student thread.
  3. Continue support/guidance conversation.

D) Assessments Management

  1. In sidebar, click Assessments.
  2. Review available assessment items/templates.
  3. Create or adopt assessment depending on current flow.
  4. Configure metadata and visibility.
  5. Publish or assign according to process.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Enrollment action button is disabled. A: Confirm permission level and request status compatibility.

item.title: Authentication and Account
item.description:
This guide covers parent login, registration, verification, setup-password, and reset-password flows.

A) Parent Login (Click-by-Click)

  1. Open parent login URL from Access URLs.
  2. Click email/username input.
  3. Enter your parent account email.
  4. Click password input.
  5. Enter password.
  6. Optional: Click eye icon to reveal password.
  7. Click Sign In.
  8. Wait for redirect to /dashboard.

Expected result:

  • Parent auth token cookie is set.
  • Parent dashboard loads.

B) Parent Registration (Click-by-Click)

  1. Open Register page.
  2. Fill email.
  3. Fill full name.
  4. Fill password.
  5. Enable consent checkbox.
  6. Click Register.
  7. Verify toast/confirmation message about email verification.
  8. Return to login.

C) Verify Email

  1. Open verification link from email.
  2. Wait for auto-verification.
  3. On success, parent is redirected to dashboard.
  4. If token invalid/expired, use resend verification.

D) Setup Password from Invitation Link

  1. Open setup-password URL with token.
  2. Wait for token validation.
  3. Enter new password (minimum length requirement applies).
  4. Confirm password.
  5. Submit.
  6. Return to login and sign in.

E) Request Password Reset

  1. Open login reset-password page.
  2. Enter parent email.
  3. Click Send Reset Link.
  4. Check inbox/spam folder.

F) Complete Password Reset

  1. Open reset link with token.
  2. Enter new password.
  3. Confirm password.
  4. Submit reset.
  5. Log in with updated password.

Common Parent Questions

Q: Login returns parents-only error. A: Credentials belong to non-parent role; use correct parent account.

item.title: Profile, Prompts, and Interests
item.description:
This guide covers advisor profile settings and advanced content/configuration modules.

A) Profile Management

  1. In sidebar, click Profile.
  2. Review personal and organization details.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Update text fields (name, bio, contact, organization data where available).
  5. Upload avatar/banner if supported.
  6. Click Save.
  7. Confirm updated preview.

B) Prompts Module

  1. In sidebar, click Prompts.
  2. Open prompt list by category/subcategory.
  3. Select prompt template to edit.
  4. Update content carefully.
  5. Validate syntax/placeholders.
  6. Save changes.
  7. Reopen to confirm version/state.

C) Interests Module

  1. In sidebar, click Interests.
  2. Review student interest records and statuses.
  3. Open detail for a student/entry.
  4. Update status or related metadata if action is available.
  5. Save and verify list refresh.

Best Practices

  • Keep prompt edits versioned and reviewed before broad usage.
  • Use profile info that matches advisor organization branding.
  • Process interests updates consistently to support recommendation quality.

Common Advisor Questions

Q: Prompt save failed. A: Check placeholder syntax and retry with valid template format.

item.title: Linked Student Guide
item.description:
This guide explains how parents link students, open student details, and unlink when needed.

A) Link a Student by Email (Click-by-Click)

  1. Sign in to Parent Dashboard.
  2. Find the Link Student form on dashboard.
  3. Click Student Email input.
  4. Enter student’s registered email.
  5. Click Send Link Request.
  6. Wait for one of these outcomes:
    • linked: student appears immediately in dashboard list.
    • pending: request registered, awaiting matching student-side action.

B) Open Linked Student

  1. In dashboard linked-student cards, click View Student.
  2. Open /dashboard/{studentId} detail page.
  3. Review summary sections (progress, recommendations, assessments, mentor/advisor activity).

C) Refresh Linked Data

  1. Click refresh action in parent dashboard or student detail page.
  2. Wait for latest API data to load.
  3. Confirm unread counts and progress values update.

D) Unlink Student

  1. Open linked student detail.
  2. Find unlink/remove access action.
  3. Confirm unlink prompt.
  4. Return to dashboard and verify student card is removed.

What Parents Can View

  • Progress summary
  • Milestone completion
  • Selected recommendation insights
  • Journey map preview/download
  • Advisor unread indicators and parent-advisor messaging context

What Parents Cannot Edit

  • Student-only private settings
  • Restricted assessment configuration
  • Student account credentials/profile ownership fields

Common Parent Questions

Q: I sent link request but student does not appear. A: Link may be pending; ask student to complete corresponding link step.

item.title: Dashboard and Linked Overview
item.description:
Parent dashboard is the main overview for all linked children.

A) Open Parent Dashboard

  1. Log in as parent.
  2. Wait for dashboard home to load.
  3. Confirm linked children cards are visible (or empty state if none).

B) Understand Dashboard Blocks

Typical sections include:

  • Linked children summary cards
  • Progress and completion snapshots
  • Recommendation and advisor activity indicators
  • Link-student request form

C) Link Student from Dashboard

  1. Find Link Student form.
  2. Enter student email.
  3. Submit request.
  4. If linked instantly, select student card.
  5. If pending, wait for student-side completion.

D) Open Student Card

  1. Click linked student card.
  2. Navigate to student detail page.
  3. Review student-specific information.

E) Refresh Parent Snapshot Data

  1. Click refresh action.
  2. Wait for loading states to complete.
  3. Confirm summary values are updated.

F) Legacy Students Route Note

If /dashboard/students is opened directly, parent app redirects back to /dashboard.

item.title: Student Detail, Journey Map, and Advisor Chat
item.description:
This guide covers the parent child-detail page, journey-map outputs, and advisor chat context.

A) Open Student Detail

  1. From parent dashboard, click linked student card.
  2. Open /dashboard/{studentId} page.
  3. Confirm student header (name/avatar/basic profile) appears.

B) Review Progress and Insights

In student detail, review:

  • Assessment progress and completion indicators
  • Recommendations summary
  • Mentor and advisor activity
  • Premium/subscription status context

C) Journey Map Preview (Click-by-Click)

  1. Open Journey Map section.
  2. Select data point filters:
    • demographics
    • education
    • psychological
    • career
    • social
    • family
    • health
  3. Click Preview.
  4. Review returned structured preview.

D) Journey Map Download

  1. Keep selected data points.
  2. Click Download/Generate Journey Map.
  3. Wait for map link generation.
  4. Open generated map link in browser.

E) Advisor Chat Indicators and Access

  1. Locate advisor chat panel/button in student detail.
  2. Check unread count badge.
  3. Open parent-advisor chat if available.
  4. Read thread and send message if enabled in panel.

F) Subscription Plans View

  1. Open subscription area on child detail.
  2. Review available plans and selected plan state.
  3. Continue purchase flow when enabled in deployment.

Common Parent Questions

Q: Journey map preview is empty. A: Ensure at least one data point is selected.

item.title: Parent Forms
item.description:
Parent panel supports dynamic form responses through /form/{responseId} routes.

A) Open Parent Form

  1. Open form link from dashboard, notification, or direct URL.
  2. Wait for form load.
  3. Confirm form title/description is visible.
  4. If organization banner exists, review context section at top.

B) Fill Form Fields (Click-by-Click)

For each question:

  1. Read label and required marker.
  2. Enter/select answer according to field type.
  3. Continue until all required fields are complete.

Supported field types include:

item.title: Admin Login and Dashboard
item.description:
Admin Login (Click-by-Click)

  1. Open the Admin dashboard login URL.
  2. Enter username or email.
  3. Enter password.
  4. Click Sign In.
  5. Wait for redirect to dashboard.

Expected result:

  • Admin auth cookie is set.
  • Dashboard loads KPI cards and recent activity.

Password Reset

  1. Open reset-password route.
  2. Enter account email.
  3. Submit reset request.
  4. Open reset link from inbox.
  5. Set new password.
  6. Log in again.

Dashboard First Checks

  1. Confirm KPI cards load.
  2. Check recent assessments table.
  3. Review registration trend chart.
  4. Verify system activity panel updates.

Token Usage and Activity

  1. Open token usage section.
  2. Select date range/interval.
  3. Review input/output token trends.
  4. Cross-check spikes with logs module.

Common Questions

Q: Login works but dashboard widgets are empty. A: Wait for API load and refresh once; verify API base URL and token validity.

item.title: User and Organization Management
item.description:
Student Management

  1. Open Student Management.
  2. Use search by name/email.
  3. Apply filters and sorting.
  4. Expand student row for full detail.
  5. Review verification, premium, and activity counters.
  6. Use delete action only after confirmation review.

Parent Management

  1. Open Parent Management.
  2. Search/filter parents.
  3. Open parent detail.
  4. Edit parent profile fields if needed.
  5. Link parent to student or unlink relationship.
  6. Send invite when required.

Organization Management

  1. Open Organization Management.
  2. Click Create Organization.
  3. Fill organization profile and primary admin details.
  4. Save and verify organization appears in list.
  5. Edit, export, or delete based on policy.

Safety Checklist Before Deletion

  1. Confirm no active dependencies.
  2. Export relevant records if needed.
  3. Use confirmation dialogs carefully.

Common Questions

Q: Parent linked but student still missing. A: Refresh relation list and verify student account exists in same environment.

item.title: Admin and Subadmin Management
item.description:
Create Admin User

  1. Open Admin Management.
  2. Click Add Admin.
  3. Fill identity fields (email, username, first/last name).
  4. Set temporary password.
  5. Choose role.
  6. Save and confirm creation.

Edit Admin Profile

  1. Open admin row detail.
  2. Update identity fields.
  3. Save updates.
  4. Confirm list refresh reflects changes.

Permission Assignment

  1. Open permission panel for target admin.
  2. Set feature scopes per module:
    • write
    • read
    • none
  3. Set organization-level scope where applicable.
  4. Save permissions.
  5. Ask user to relogin if permissions do not refresh immediately.

Subadmin Self View

  1. Open Subadmin page.
  2. Review effective permission summary.
  3. Use refresh permissions action to sync latest grants.

Common Questions

Q: Admin can see module but cannot perform action. A: Verify action-level permission is write, not read.

item.title: Assessments and Forms
item.description:
Assessments Module

  1. Open Assessments.
  2. Review existing assessments.
  3. Click Create Assessment.
  4. Add title and description.
  5. Add questions and select question types.
  6. Configure options and required fields.
  7. Save and test.

Supported assessment question patterns include rating, multiple choice, short answer, and yes/no.

Assessment Upload Flow

  1. Open Upload action.
  2. Select CSV/XLSX file.
  3. Validate import preview/errors.
  4. Confirm import.

Forms Module

  1. Open Forms list.
  2. Create or open existing form.
  3. Edit form structure/content.
  4. Save draft or publish.
  5. Open responses view to review submissions.
  6. Open individual response detail when needed.

Common Questions

Q: Imported assessment has broken options. A: Verify file schema and option label columns before import.

item.title: Prompts, Translations, and Content
item.description:
Prompts Management

  1. Open Prompts.
  2. Select category.
  3. Select subcategory.
  4. Open target prompt template.
  5. Edit text in editor.
  6. Validate syntax.
  7. Save and verify version update.

Prompt Quality Checks

  1. Confirm variable placeholders are valid.
  2. Review lint/syntax errors.
  3. Review diff between previous and current versions.

Translations Management

  1. Open Translations.
  2. Filter by namespace and locale.
  3. Search by key or value.
  4. Edit existing translation.
  5. Create missing translation keys where needed.
  6. Save and validate in target UI route.

Mentor Personality and Interests

  1. Open Mentor Personality.
  2. Edit localized mentor fields and expertise arrays.
  3. Save and verify list updates.
  4. Open Interests module.
  5. Review status queue and update where allowed.

Common Questions

Q: Prompt save fails with template error. A: Fix invalid placeholder/template syntax and retry.

item.title: Support, Notifications, and Approvals
item.description:
Support Tickets

  1. Open Support Tickets.
  2. Filter by status and priority.
  3. Open ticket detail.
  4. Update status.
  5. Add resolution message.
  6. Save and verify status transition.

Notifications

  1. Open Notifications.
  2. Click Create Notification.
  3. Enter title and message.
  4. Select target type (all, users, groups).
  5. Select recipients if needed.
  6. Send and verify history entry.

Demo Approvals and Admission Criteria

  1. Open Demo Approvals queue.
  2. Review request details.
  3. Approve or reject with notes where required.
  4. Open Admission Criteria module.
  5. Edit or review criteria entries.
  6. Save and verify publishing/active state.

Common Questions

Q: Ticket transitions fail silently. A: Reopen ticket and check required resolution fields before save.

item.title: System Monitoring and Governance
item.description:
Status Module

  1. Open Status page.
  2. Review service health blocks.
  3. Identify degraded components.
  4. Correlate with recent alerts.

Realtime Logs

  1. Open Realtime Logs.
  2. Start live stream view.
  3. Filter by level/source if available.
  4. Track suspicious error bursts.

Free-Use Management

  1. Open Free Use Management.
  2. Review current quota/limit values.
  3. Update limits according to policy.
  4. Save and verify effect on applicable feature gates.

Certificates and Application Documents

  1. Open Certificates.
  2. Review issuance queue/status.
  3. Open Application Documents.
  4. Review document validations and statuses.
  5. Apply review decisions and notes.

Common Questions

Q: Logs show spikes but dashboard looks normal. A: Compare time windows and cross-check token usage metrics.
